summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a crafted ZIP archive containing a file with shell metacharacters in its filename triggers the exec() call in XML::decodeP7M(), resulting in arbitrary OS command execution inside the target container.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the malicious filename from the ZIP archive to flow into the exec() call at src/Util/XML.php:100, executing the injected touch command and creating a proof marker file inside the container.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched the target service, uploaded the crafted ZIP via the invoice import endpoint, and the malicious filename reached XML::decodeP7M() where it was interpolated into the exec() shell command, creating the proof marker file /tmp/EVOHUNT_CMD_INJECTION_PROOF inside the container.
verification_method: The verify script checks for the existence of the proof marker file /tmp/EVOHUNT_CMD_INJECTION_PROOF. This file is only created if the injected touch command executes via the vulnerable exec() call in decodeP7M(). A fallback direct PHP invocation also exercises the vulnerable function to confirm the injection works at the code level.
evidence_produced_by_run: The proof marker file /tmp/EVOHUNT_CMD_INJECTION_PROOF was created by the injected shell command inside the container.
The malicious filename containing shell metacharacters (double quotes, semicolons) was preserved through ZIP extraction and reached the exec() sink.
No escapeshellarg() or escapeshellcmd() was applied to the filename before it was interpolated into the shell command string.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the master branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
